message:
feat(demo): implement Performance HUD screen with real-time metrics

Add full-featured PerformanceHud screen with tick interval measurement,
FPS estimation via views-per-tick EMA, latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99),
braille sparkline visualization, render budget tracking, and degradation
tier indicators. Includes 18 unit tests covering all interactions.

Closes bd-3k3x.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.4","title":"Render Pipeline: Atomic Reflow on Resize","description":"## Background

Resize is where TUIs go to die: partial paints, torn frames, and stale content (ghost rows) are the classic failure modes.

FrankenTUI's design philosophy (determinism, one-writer rule, RAII teardown) implies resize must be handled as a first-class *transaction*:
- a resize event results in exactly one coherent present
- any cleanup required by shrink happens in the same transaction
- presenter state is consistent after the transaction (no latent cursor/SGR state)

This task is the top-level "make resize reflow real" work item for the render pipeline.

## Goal

Implement **atomic reflow on resize** in both screen modes:
- Alt-screen: full-screen UI
- Inline: stable UI region with scrollback preserved

Atomic means: from the terminal's point of view, each resize produces a single, consistent frame without intermediate corrupted states.

## Scope

1) Replace placeholder resize behavior with continuous reflow.
- The runtime must produce a view at the new size (layout + widgets) and present it.

2) Define and enforce an **atomic present protocol** (see bd-1rz0.4.1).
- Clear/shrink cleanup happens before/with drawing.
- Output is one coherent write per frame (or as close as the writer can enforce).

3) Ensure **shrink cleanup** prevents ghosting.
- When height decreases, rows that were previously painted but are now out-of-bounds must be erased.
- When UI height changes (inline mode), stale chrome rows must be erased.

4) Presenter / terminal writer state correctness.
- Cursor position, SGR state, scroll region, and saved cursor semantics must not leak across the resize transaction.
- Any capability-dependent behavior (sync output, cursor save/restore) must follow deterministic fallback rules.

## Design Notes (Implementation Guidance)

- Treat resize handling as a transaction boundary:
  - compute new Frame/Buffer
  - compute Diff against previous Buffer
  - run shrink cleanup (if needed)
  - emit ANSI as one present

- Ghost row cleanup must be *dimension-aware*:
  - track previous terminal size
  - compute the "rows to erase" region as: [new_height .. prev_height)
  - clamp all erase operations to the actual terminal height

- Inline mode needs special care:
  - preserve scrollback above UI
  - only touch the reserved UI region + any ghost rows below it

## Tests / Validation

- Unit tests:
  - shrink: when height decreases, the presenter/terminal writer emits erases for ghost rows
  - grow: no unnecessary full clears
  - state reset: SGR/cursor assumptions after resize are correct

- Snapshot tests:
  - scripted resize sequences (grow -> shrink -> grow) with stable expected output

- PTY E2E:
  - resize storms at fixed seed and fixed schedule
  - verbose JSONL logs (env/caps/seed/timings/checksums)
  - checksum validation for final rendered frame

## Acceptance Criteria

- No ghosting after shrink in alt-screen and inline modes.
- Reflow is atomic: no visible intermediate corruption during resize sequences.
- Determinism: given the same input events and seed, produced output checksums match.
- Performance: resize handling stays within an explicit budget (capture p50/p95/p99 and add a regression gate in bd-1rz0.11).

## Alien Artifact Upgrades

- Explainability: when a capability fallback is used during resize present (e.g., no sync output), record a minimal evidence ledger entry (what capability, what evidence, what fallback).
- Guarantees: incorporate resize SLA monitoring (bd-1rz0.26) with anytime-valid alerts.

## Extreme Optimization

- Baseline the resize-storm PTY scenario (p50/p95/p99) before micro-optimizations.
- Only optimize after profiling; one lever per change; preserve behavior via golden frame checksums.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:23.240008932Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:52:20.953192761Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:52:20.953164467Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rz0.4.1","title":"Atomic Present Protocol + Shrink Cleanup","description":"## Background

This task defines the **atomic present protocol** used by the render pipeline to make resize safe:
- avoid partial paints during resize transactions
- ensure shrink cleanup happens immediately (no ghost rows)
- keep terminal writer / presenter state coherent after every present

This is the contract between runtime->render (BufferDiff) and output (TerminalWriter/Presenter).

## Protocol (Normative)

A resize-triggered present must behave like a transaction:

1) Determine current terminal size and update prev size tracking.
- Previous size is required for shrink cleanup.

2) If the terminal height shrank, erase any rows that were previously painted but are now out-of-bounds.
- Alt-screen: erase rows in the alt buffer beyond new height.
- Inline: erase ghost rows below the UI region, clamped to the real terminal height.

3) Ensure presenter state is safe for the new frame.
- Cursor position and SGR state should not leak across the transaction.

4) Present the diff as one coherent output batch.
- Prefer synchronized output when supported.
- Preserve one-writer invariants and avoid interleaving.

5) Restore any cursor-save invariants required by inline mode.

## Shrink Cleanup Rules

- Ghost rows are the set of rows that were previously inside the painted region but are now outside the new buffer height.
- Cleanup must be bounded and deterministic:
  - erase only the necessary region
  - clamp coordinates to current terminal size

## Tests / Validation

- Unit tests:
  - prev_term_width/prev_term_height tracking updates correctly
  - shrink cleanup erases the correct rows
  - inline mode clamps ghost-row cleanup to terminal height

- Snapshot tests:
  - shrink/grow sequences produce stable output

## Acceptance Criteria

- Protocol is documented and enforceable (module docs + tests).
- No residual artifacts after shrink in either screen mode.
- Determinism: stable behavior under fixed resize event sequences.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.068647193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:27:27.147022879Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:27:27.146654034Z","close_reason":"Implemented atomic present protocol + shrink cleanup; tests added (see issue comment).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","render"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":338,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Atomic present protocol implemented with shrink cleanup. ## Background
Bracketed paste mode prevents pasted content from being interpreted as commands. SGR mouse protocol provides precise mouse reporting with modifiers.

}

pub enum InputEvent {
    Key(KeyEvent),
    Mouse(MouseEvent),
    Paste(String),  // Complete paste content
    Resize(u16, u16),
    Focus(bool),
}

### SGR Mouse Protocol (1006)
Escape sequence: ESC[<Cb;Cx;CyM or ESC[<Cb;Cx;Cym

struct SgrMouseParser;

impl SgrMouseParser {
    /// Parse SGR extended mouse report
    pub fn parse(&self, params: &[u8]) -> Option<MouseEvent> {
        // Parse: <button;column;rowM or <button;column;rowm
        // Button encoding:
        //   0 = left, 1 = middle, 2 = right
        //   +4 = shift, +8 = meta, +16 = ctrl
        //   32 = motion, 64 = scroll
        // M = press, m = release
    }
}

pub struct MouseEvent {
    pub kind: MouseEventKind,
    pub column: u16,
    pub row: u16,
    pub modifiers: KeyModifiers,
}

pub enum MouseEventKind {
    Down(MouseButton),
    Up(MouseButton),
    Drag(MouseButton),
    Moved,
    ScrollDown,
    ScrollUp,
    ScrollLeft,
    ScrollRight,
}

### Enable/Disable Mouse
pub fn enable_mouse_capture(writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
    // Enable SGR extended mode (1006)
    // Enable any-event tracking (1003) or button-event (1002)
    write!(writer, "\x1b[?1000h\x1b[?1006h")?;
    Ok(())
}

pub fn disable_mouse_capture(writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
    write!(writer, "\x1b[?1006l\x1b[?1000l")?;
    Ok(())
}

### Paste Size Limits
- Default max: 1MB
- Configurable via builder
- Large pastes: return error or truncate

### Focus Events (bonus)
- Enable: ESC[?1004h
- Disable: ESC[?1004l
- Focus in: ESC[I
- Focus out: ESC[O

- [ ] SGR mouse position decoding
- [ ] Modifier key detection
- [ ] Scroll events
- [ ] Motion events

- [ ] Focus in/out events

 {"id":"bd-3j8t","title":"Implement Virtualized<T> container with visible range calculation","description":"# Implement Virtualized<T> container with visible range calculation

## Background
Part of Progressive Rendering (bd-3t87). The core container that tracks scroll position and calculates which items are visible.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Virtualized content container
pub struct Virtualized<T> {
    /// All items (owned or reference to external storage)
    items: VirtualizedStorage<T>,
    
    /// Current scroll offset (in items)
    scroll_offset: usize,
    
    /// Number of visible items (computed from area height)
    visible_count: usize,
    
    /// Overscan: extra items rendered above/below visible
    overscan: usize,
    
    /// Height per item
    item_height: ItemHeight,
    
    /// Whether to auto-scroll on new items
    follow_mode: bool,
    
    /// Scroll velocity for momentum
    scroll_velocity: f32,
}

/// Storage strategy for items
pub enum VirtualizedStorage<T> {
    /// Owned vector of items
    Owned(Vec<T>),
    
    /// External storage with fetch function
    External {
        len: usize,
        fetch: Box<dyn Fn(Range<usize>) -> Vec<T>>,
    },
}

/// Height calculation strategy
pub enum ItemHeight {
    /// All items have fixed height
    Fixed(u16),
    
    /// Items have variable height, cached
    Variable(HeightCache),
}

/// LRU cache for measured item heights
pub struct HeightCache {
    cache: LruCache<usize, u16>,
    default_height: u16,
}

impl<T> Virtualized<T> {
    /// Create with owned storage
    pub fn new(items: Vec<T>) -> Self {
        Self {
            items: VirtualizedStorage::Owned(items),
            scroll_offset: 0,
            visible_count: 0,
            overscan: 2,
            item_height: ItemHeight::Fixed(1),
            follow_mode: false,
            scroll_velocity: 0.0,
        }
    }
    
    /// Create with external storage
    pub fn external<F>(len: usize, fetch: F) -> Self
    where
        F: Fn(Range<usize>) -> Vec<T> + 'static,
    {
        Self {
            items: VirtualizedStorage::External { len, fetch: Box::new(fetch) },
            scroll_offset: 0,
            visible_count: 0,
            overscan: 2,
            item_height: ItemHeight::Fixed(1),
            follow_mode: false,
            scroll_velocity: 0.0,
        }
    }
    
    /// Total number of items
    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
        match &self.items {
            VirtualizedStorage::Owned(v) => v.len(),
            VirtualizedStorage::External { len, .. } => *len,
        }
    }
    
    /// Calculate visible range given viewport height
    pub fn visible_range(&self, viewport_height: u16) -> Range<usize> {
        let items_per_screen = match &self.item_height {
            ItemHeight::Fixed(h) => (viewport_height / h) as usize,
            ItemHeight::Variable(cache) => {
                // Sum heights until we exceed viewport
                let mut count = 0;
                let mut total_height = 0u16;
                let start = self.scroll_offset;
                while total_height < viewport_height && start + count < self.len() {
                    total_height += cache.get(start + count);
                    count += 1;
                }
                count
            }
        };
        
        self.visible_count = items_per_screen;
        
        let start = self.scroll_offset;
        let end = (start + items_per_screen).min(self.len());
        start..end
    }
    
    /// Get range with overscan for smooth scrolling
    pub fn render_range(&self, viewport_height: u16) -> Range<usize> {
        let visible = self.visible_range(viewport_height);
        let start = visible.start.saturating_sub(self.overscan);
        let end = (visible.end + self.overscan).min(self.len());
        start..end
    }
    
    /// Get item by index
    pub fn get(&self, idx: usize) -> Option<&T> {
        match &self.items {
            VirtualizedStorage::Owned(v) => v.get(idx),
            VirtualizedStorage::External { .. } => None, // Must use fetch
        }
    }
    
    /// Update external storage length
    pub fn set_len(&mut self, len: usize) {
        if let VirtualizedStorage::External { len: ref mut l, .. } = &mut self.items {
            *l = len;
        }
    }
}

impl HeightCache {
    pub fn new(default_height: u16, capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LruCache::new(NonZeroUsize::new(capacity).unwrap()),
            default_height,
        }
    }
    
    pub fn get(&self, idx: usize) -> u16 {
        *self.cache.get(&idx).unwrap_or(&self.default_height)
    }
    
    pub fn set(&mut self, idx: usize, height: u16) {
        self.cache.put(idx, height);
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3t87","title":"Progressive Rendering for Large Content with Virtualization","description":"# Progressive Rendering for Large Content with Virtualization

## Background & Motivation

Agent harnesses accumulate massive amounts of data over long sessions:
- Tool outputs can be thousands of lines
- LLM responses stream continuously
- Log files grow unbounded

Naively rendering all content causes O(n) render time, making the UI unusable.

**Why this matters:**
- Agent sessions can run for hours, accumulating 100K+ log lines
- Users expect smooth scrolling regardless of content size
- Memory usage must be bounded
- Render time must be O(visible) not O(total)

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Enhances bd-29v (Viewport/LogViewer) with virtualization
- Works with bd-1iqn (Render Budget) for graceful degradation

## Technical Design

### Core Types

```rust
/// Virtualized content container
pub struct Virtualized<T> {
    /// All items (or reference to external storage)
    items: VirtualizedStorage<T>,
    /// Current scroll offset (in items)
    scroll_offset: usize,
    /// Number of visible items (computed from area height)
    visible_count: usize,
    /// Overscan: extra items rendered above/below visible
    overscan: usize,
    /// Height per item (fixed or measured)
    item_height: ItemHeight,
    /// Scroll velocity for momentum
    scroll_velocity: f32,
}

pub enum VirtualizedStorage<T> {
    /// Owned vec of items
    Owned(Vec<T>),
    /// Reference to external storage with len
    External { len: usize, fetch: Box<dyn Fn(Range<usize>) -> Vec<T>> },
}

pub enum ItemHeight {
    /// All items have fixed height
    Fixed(u16),
    /// Items have variable height, measured lazily
    Variable(HeightCache),
}

/// LRU cache for measured item heights
pub struct HeightCache {
    cache: LruCache<usize, u16>,
    default_height: u16,
}
```

### Virtualized List Widget

```rust
impl<T: RenderItem> Widget for VirtualizedList<T> {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        // Calculate visible range
        let (start, end) = self.visible_range(area.height);
        
        // Apply overscan
        let render_start = start.saturating_sub(self.overscan);
        let render_end = (end + self.overscan).min(self.len());
        
        // Render only visible items
        let mut y = area.y;
        for idx in render_start..render_end {
            if let Some(item) = self.get(idx) {
                let item_height = self.item_height(idx);
                let item_area = Rect::new(area.x, y, area.width, item_height);
                
                if item_area.bottom() > area.y && item_area.top() < area.bottom() {
                    item.render(item_area, buf);
                }
                
                y += item_height;
            }
        }
        
        // Render scrollbar
        if self.len() > self.visible_count {
            self.render_scrollbar(area, buf);
        }
    }
}

impl<T> VirtualizedList<T> {
    fn visible_range(&self, height: u16) -> (usize, usize) {
        let start = self.scroll_offset;
        let visible = match &self.item_height {
            ItemHeight::Fixed(h) => (height / h) as usize,
            ItemHeight::Variable(cache) => {
                // Sum heights until we exceed viewport
                let mut count = 0;
                let mut total_height = 0u16;
                while total_height < height && start + count < self.len() {
                    total_height += cache.get_or_default(start + count);
                    count += 1;
                }
                count
            }
        };
        (start, start + visible)
    }
}
```

### Scroll Handling

```rust
impl<T> VirtualizedList<T> {
    /// Scroll by delta (positive = down)
    pub fn scroll(&mut self, delta: i32) {
        let new_offset = (self.scroll_offset as i32 + delta)
            .max(0)
            .min(self.len().saturating_sub(1) as i32);
        self.scroll_offset = new_offset as usize;
    }
    
    /// Scroll to specific item
    pub fn scroll_to(&mut self, idx: usize) {
        self.scroll_offset = idx.min(self.len().saturating_sub(1));
    }
    
    /// Scroll to bottom (follow mode for logs)
    pub fn scroll_to_bottom(&mut self) {
        if self.len() > self.visible_count {
            self.scroll_offset = self.len() - self.visible_count;
        }
    }
```

### Memory-Efficient Log Storage

```rust
/// Log storage with bounded memory and optional persistence
pub struct LogRing {
    /// Circular buffer of log lines
    ring: VecDeque<LogLine>,
    /// Maximum lines in memory
    capacity: usize,
    /// Total lines ever received (for accurate scroll positions)
    total_count: usize,
    /// Optional overflow file for persistence
    overflow: Option<OverflowFile>,
}

impl LogRing {
    pub fn push(&mut self, line: LogLine) {
        self.total_count += 1;
        
        if self.ring.len() >= self.capacity {
            if let Some(overflow) = &mut self.overflow {
                // Write evicted line to overflow file
                overflow.write(self.ring.pop_front().unwrap());
            } else {
                self.ring.pop_front();
            }
        }
        
        self.ring.push_back(line);
    }
    
    pub fn get(&self, idx: usize) -> Option<&LogLine> {
        // Map absolute index to ring index
        let ring_start = self.total_count.saturating_sub(self.ring.len());
        if idx >= ring_start && idx < self.total_count {
            self.ring.get(idx - ring_start)
        } else if let Some(overflow) = &self.overflow {
            // Read from overflow (may involve I/O)
            overflow.read(idx)
        } else {
            None
        }
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3tfu","title":"Implement RenderBudget struct with time tracking","description":"# Implement RenderBudget struct with time tracking

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). The RenderBudget struct tracks elapsed time during frame rendering and manages degradation level.

## Technical Design

```rust
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

/// Render time budget with graceful degradation
pub struct RenderBudget {
    /// Total time budget for this frame
    total: Duration,
    /// When this frame started rendering
    start: Instant,
    /// Current degradation level
    degradation: DegradationLevel,
    /// Per-phase budgets (optional breakdown)
    phases: PhaseBudgets,
}

/// Budget allocation per render phase
pub struct PhaseBudgets {
    /// Budget for widget tree traversal
    pub traverse: Duration,
    /// Budget for diff computation
    pub diff: Duration,
    /// Budget for ANSI emission
    pub present: Duration,
}

impl RenderBudget {
    /// Create a budget with the given total time
    pub fn new(total: Duration) -> Self {
        Self {
            total,
            start: Instant::now(),
            degradation: DegradationLevel::Full,
            phases: PhaseBudgets::default(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Time remaining in this frame budget
    #[inline]
    pub fn remaining(&self) -> Duration {
        self.total.saturating_sub(self.start.elapsed())
    }
    
    /// Check if we should degrade before an expensive operation
    /// estimated_cost: rough estimate of how long the operation takes
    #[inline]
    pub fn should_degrade(&self, estimated_cost: Duration) -> bool {
        self.remaining() < estimated_cost
    }
    
    /// Advance to next degradation level
    pub fn degrade(&mut self) {
        let old = self.degradation;
        self.degradation = self.degradation.degrade();
        tracing::warn!(
            from = ?old,
            to = ?self.degradation,
            elapsed_ms = self.start.elapsed().as_millis(),
            "Degrading render fidelity"
        );
    }
    
    /// Get current degradation level for widgets to check
    #[inline]
    pub fn degradation(&self) -> DegradationLevel {
        self.degradation
    }
    
    /// Check if budget is exhausted (should skip frame)
    #[inline]
    pub fn exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        self.remaining().is_zero() || self.degradation.skip_frame()
    }
    
    /// Record phase entry for detailed timing
    pub fn enter_phase(&mut self, phase: RenderPhase) {
        // For tracing spans
    }
    
    /// Get elapsed time since frame start
    #[inline]
    pub fn elapsed(&self) -> Duration {
        self.start.elapsed()
    }
}

impl Default for PhaseBudgets {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            traverse: Duration::from_millis(4),
            diff: Duration::from_millis(2),
            present: Duration::from_millis(4),
        }
    }
}
```

## Why These Defaults
- Total 16ms = ~60fps feel
- 4ms traverse: widget tree can be complex
- 2ms diff: typically fast with good cell comparison
- 4ms present: includes IO overhead
- Remaining ~6ms: buffer for variance

## Test Requirements
- [ ] remaining() decreases as time passes
- [ ] should_degrade() returns true when cost > remaining
- [ ] exhausted() when remaining is zero OR degradation is SkipFrame
- [ ] degrade() logs warning via tracing

- [ ] PhaseBudgets with sensible defaults
- [ ] Time tracking is accurate

## Background & Motivation

Terminal UIs benefit from subtle animations:
- Spinner for loading states
- Fade for transitions
- Pulse for attention

**The solution:**
- Composable animation primitives
- Budget-aware (skip frames under load)

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-1iqn (Render Budget) for graceful degradation
- Used by widgets like Spinner, Progress

## Technical Design

```rust
pub trait Animation {
    fn tick(&mut self, dt: Duration);
    fn is_complete(&self) -> bool;
    fn value(&self) -> f32;  // 0.0 to 1.0
}

pub struct Fade { progress: f32, duration: Duration }
pub struct Slide { from: i16, to: i16, progress: f32, duration: Duration }
pub struct Pulse { frequency: f32, phase: f32 }

// Easing functions
pub fn ease_in(t: f32) -> f32 { t * t }
pub fn ease_out(t: f32) -> f32 { 1.0 - (1.0 - t) * (1.0 - t) }
pub fn ease_in_out(t: f32) -> f32 { ... }

// Composition
pub fn sequence(a: impl Animation, b: impl Animation) -> impl Animation { ... }
pub fn parallel(a: impl Animation, b: impl Animation) -> impl Animation { ... }
pub fn delay(d: Duration, a: impl Animation) -> impl Animation { ... }
```

Animations must handle edge cases gracefully:

```rust
impl Fade {
    pub fn new(duration: Duration) -> Self {
        // Handle zero duration
        let duration = if duration.is_zero() {
            Duration::from_millis(1)  // Minimum 1ms
        } else {
            duration
        };
        Self { progress: 0.0, duration }
    }
}

impl Animation for Fade {
    fn tick(&mut self, dt: Duration) {
        // Clamp progress to [0, 1]
        let delta = dt.as_secs_f32() / self.duration.as_secs_f32();
        self.progress = (self.progress + delta).clamp(0.0, 1.0);
    }
    
    fn value(&self) -> f32 {
        self.progress  // Already clamped
    }
}

// Easing functions must handle edge cases
pub fn ease_in(t: f32) -> f32 {
    t.clamp(0.0, 1.0).powi(2)  // Clamp input
}
```

## Requirements
- BiDi level resolution per character
- Visual reordering of runs
- Bracket pairing algorithm
- Explicit direction markers support

## Proposed API

```rust
use unicode_bidi::{BidiInfo, Level};

/// BiDi-aware text segment.
///
/// Notes:
/// - Indices are in logical *character* space (not bytes).
/// - Rendering should not use O(n^2) `chars().nth()` lookups; precompute index maps.
pub struct BidiSegment {
    pub text: String,
    pub chars: Vec<char>,
    pub levels: Vec<Level>,
    pub runs: Vec<BidiRun>,
    pub visual_to_logical: Vec<usize>,
    pub logical_to_visual: Vec<usize>,
}

/// A run of text with consistent direction
pub struct BidiRun {
    pub start: usize,
    pub end: usize,
    pub level: Level,
    pub direction: Direction,
}

impl BidiSegment {
    /// Create from text with optional base direction
    pub fn new(text: &str, base: Option<Direction>) -> Self {
        let bidi_info = BidiInfo::new(text, base.map(|d| d.into()));
        let paragraph = bidi_info.paragraphs.first().unwrap();

        // Pseudocode: build Vec<char> once, then build maps once.
        // visual_to_logical is a permutation of 0..chars.len().

        Self {
            text: text.to_string(),
            chars: text.chars().collect(),
            levels: bidi_info.levels.clone(),
            runs: Self::compute_runs(&bidi_info, paragraph),
            visual_to_logical: Self::compute_visual_map(&bidi_info, paragraph),
            logical_to_visual: Self::invert_map(&visual_to_logical),
        }
    }

    /// Get visual position of logical index
    pub fn visual_pos(&self, logical: usize) -> usize {
        self.logical_to_visual[logical]
    }

    /// Get logical position of visual index
    pub fn logical_pos(&self, visual: usize) -> usize {
        self.visual_to_logical[visual]
    }

    /// Check if logical position is RTL
    pub fn is_rtl(&self, logical: usize) -> bool {
        self.levels[logical].is_rtl()
    }

    /// Move cursor right (visual)
    pub fn move_right(&self, logical: usize) -> usize {
        let visual = self.visual_pos(logical);
        if visual + 1 < self.visual_to_logical.len() {
            self.logical_pos(visual + 1)
        } else {
            logical
        }
    }

    /// Move cursor left (visual)
    pub fn move_left(&self, logical: usize) -> usize {
        let visual = self.visual_pos(logical);
        if visual > 0 {
            self.logical_pos(visual - 1)
        } else {
            logical
        }
    }
}
```

## Proposed API

```rust
use unicode_bidi::{BidiInfo, Level};

/// BiDi-aware text segment.
///
/// Notes:
/// - Indices are in logical *character* space (not bytes).
/// - Rendering should not use O(n^2) `chars().nth()` lookups; precompute index maps.
pub struct BidiSegment {
    pub text: String,
    pub chars: Vec<char>,
    pub levels: Vec<Level>,
    pub runs: Vec<BidiRun>,
    pub visual_to_logical: Vec<usize>,
    pub logical_to_visual: Vec<usize>,
}

/// A run of text with consistent direction
pub struct BidiRun {
    pub start: usize,
    pub end: usize,
    pub level: Level,
    pub direction: Direction,
}

impl BidiSegment {
    /// Create from text with optional base direction
    pub fn new(text: &str, base: Option<Direction>) -> Self {
        let bidi_info = BidiInfo::new(text, base.map(|d| d.into()));
        let paragraph = bidi_info.paragraphs.first().unwrap();

        // Pseudocode: build Vec<char> once, then build maps once.
        // visual_to_logical is a permutation of 0..chars.len().

        Self {
            text: text.to_string(),
            chars: text.chars().collect(),
            levels: bidi_info.levels.clone(),
            runs: Self::compute_runs(&bidi_info, paragraph),
            visual_to_logical: Self::compute_visual_map(&bidi_info, paragraph),
            logical_to_visual: Self::invert_map(&visual_to_logical),
        }
    }

    /// Get visual position of logical index
    pub fn visual_pos(&self, logical: usize) -> usize {
        self.logical_to_visual[logical]
    }

    /// Get logical position of visual index
    pub fn logical_pos(&self, visual: usize) -> usize {
        self.visual_to_logical[visual]
    }

    /// Check if logical position is RTL
    pub fn is_rtl(&self, logical: usize) -> bool {
        self.levels[logical].is_rtl()
    }

    /// Move cursor right (visual)
    pub fn move_right(&self, logical: usize) -> usize {
        let visual = self.visual_pos(logical);
        if visual + 1 < self.visual_to_logical.len() {
            self.logical_pos(visual + 1)
        } else {
            logical
        }
    }

    /// Move cursor left (visual)
    pub fn move_left(&self, logical: usize) -> usize {
        let visual = self.visual_pos(logical);
        if visual > 0 {
            self.logical_pos(visual - 1)
        } else {
            logical
        }
    }
}
```
